fields.description:
# S4a — Review-bot Gerrit identity + event plumbing (plugins, SSM token, IAM)

## Why
Before the review-bot can vote (S4b), the Gerrit-side plumbing must exist: a service-account
identity the bot votes as, the event-delivery plugins that notify it, and the secrets/IAM to
reach them. Split out from the bot's review/vote LOGIC (S4b) because this is a distinct layer
(Gerrit account + plugin install + AWS IAM/SSM) with no application logic — keeping each story
single-layered (the S4 oversize the gate flagged was 4 layers in one ticket).

## What
- **Bot identity.** A dedicated Gerrit account in the built-in `Service Users` group (thread
  isolation; SERVICE_USER accounts are excluded from the attention set except on a negative
  vote). Its HTTP auth token (for the OUTBOUND REST vote in S4b) is GENERATED by
  `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` itself — after creating the account it calls
  `PUT /a/accounts/<bot>/password.http` (the account HTTP-password / token endpoint), captures
  the value returned in the response body, and stores it in SSM Parameter Store at a fixed path
  (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`). `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` is **idempotent**:
  check-before-create (`GET /a/accounts/<bot>`) and `put-parameter --overwrite`, so
  re-provisioning regenerates+restores the token without duplicating the account or orphaning it.
- **Least-privilege IAM.** The instance role grants `ssm:GetParameter` scoped to exactly the bot
  parameter path (`arn:aws:ssm:<region>:<acct>:parameter/rebar/prod/*`), never `Resource:"*"`.
- **Event delivery — two integrated channels.**
  - *Push (primary):* install the `webhooks` plugin to POST `patchset-created` to the review-bot
    receiver (the HTTP endpoint stood up in S2, behavior wired in S4b).
  - *Backfill (safety net):* install the `events-log` plugin, which STORES a queryable event log
    (`GET /a/plugins/events-log/events?...`) — it does NOT auto-replay; the S4b reconciler polls
    it. Plugin **provenance is a durable end-state, not a research note**: the exact arm64 jar
    URLs + checksums + versions for Gerrit 3.14.1 are pinned in `infra/gerrit/` (recorded files),
    so a rebuild installs byte-identical plugins.
- **Inbound webhook auth (peer-pattern correction — the `webhooks` plugin has NO HMAC).** The
  Gerrit `webhooks` plugin offers no request signing/HMAC (only `sslVerify`, which defaults
  false), and it reads its remote config EXCLUSIVELY from `refs/meta/config`. So inbound
  authenticity is bolt-on and split across the boundary. `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config` is a
  **TEMPLATE** committed with a placeholder (e.g. `remote.<name>.url =
  https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/review/?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) — NOT a static committed
  secret. At config-push time `service-user.sh` reads the token from SSM, TEMPLATES the URL by
  substituting the placeholder, and pushes the rendered config to `refs/meta/config`. Because the
  `webhooks` plugin only reads `refs/meta/config`, a URL-embedded token NECESSARILY lands there —
  so the URL-token is NOT "kept out of refs/meta/config." It is a SECONDARY, bounded-exposure
  control: `refs/meta/config` is Gerrit-access-controlled and is NOT replicated to GitHub
  (`replicatePermissions=false`), and the token is rotatable. The PRIMARY inbound control is the
  **network ACL** — the `/review/` path on nginx is reachable only from the Gerrit host
  (loopback/SG). S4b's receiver additionally validates the token + source.

## Scope
In: the Gerrit Service-Users bot account + SSM token + least-privilege IAM + install/pin the
`webhooks` and `events-log` plugins + the inbound webhook secret/ACL config. Out: the receiver's
parse/review/vote/reconcile LOGIC and observability (S4b); replication (S5). Depends on S3
(project + `LLM-Review` label/ACL exist so the bot's vote permission resolves) and S2 (the
receiver service + nginx `/review/` route exist).

## Files
- `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` — idempotent bot-account creation; GENERATES the bot HTTP token
  via `PUT /a/accounts/<bot>/password.http`, captures it, and upserts it to SSM; at config-push
  time renders `webhooks.config` from its template (token from SSM) and pushes to
  `refs/meta/config`.
- `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config` — `webhooks` plugin remote config TEMPLATE: a placeholder
  secret-token URL (patchset-created -> the `/review/` receiver) + `allowedUrlPattern` if used;
  rendered with the SSM token at push time (no committed secret).
- `infra/gerrit/plugins/` — pinned `webhooks` + `events-log` jar provenance (URLs + checksums +
  versions for 3.14.1/arm64) and the install step.
- the least-privilege SSM IAM policy for the bot token path (in the infra Terraform).

> Record `set_file_impact` for these paths before claiming.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The bot account exists in `Service Users` (SERVICE_USER tag; excluded from the attention
      set except on a negative vote); its REST auth token is GENERATED by `service-user.sh` via
      `PUT /a/accounts/<bot>/password.http`, captured, and stored in SSM
      (`/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); re-running `service-user.sh` is idempotent (no duplicate
      account / orphaned token).
- [ ] The instance role's SSM read is scoped to the bot token path only (no `Resource:"*"`),
      verified against the attached policy.
- [ ] The `webhooks` and `events-log` plugins are installed on Gerrit 3.14.1 from pinned,
      checksum-verified, arm64-compatible jars whose URLs/checksums/versions are recorded in
      `infra/gerrit/` (a rebuild reinstalls byte-identical plugins).
- [ ] `infra/gerrit/webhooks.config` is committed as a TEMPLATE with a token placeholder (no
      committed secret); `service-user.sh` renders it with the SSM token at config-push time and
      pushes it to `refs/meta/config` (the only place the `webhooks` plugin reads remote config).
- [ ] The `webhooks` remote POSTs `patchset-created` to the `/review/` receiver via the rendered
      secret-token URL, and — since the token necessarily resides in `refs/meta/config` and the
      plugin provides no HMAC — the PRIMARY control is that `/review/` is reachable only from the
      Gerrit host (network ACL / nginx): verified by a request without the token / from a
      disallowed source being refused at the edge. (`refs/meta/config` is Gerrit-access-controlled
      and not replicated to GitHub via `replicatePermissions=false`; the token is rotatable.)
- [ ] `events-log` is installed and queryable (the S4b reconciler depends on it); confirmed by a
      sample `GET /a/plugins/events-log/events` returning recent events.

## Round-7 remediation
- **Non-clobbering refs/meta/config push (T4):** `service-user.sh` updates `refs/meta/config` via **read-modify-write** (fetch current ref -> update only the webhooks config -> commit -> push), NEVER a full-tree replacement (which would wipe S3's `LLM-Review` label / submit-requirement / ACL on `refs/meta/config`).
- [ ] The webhooks-config push is read-modify-write and preserves S3's existing `refs/meta/config` (verified: after S4a's push the `LLM-Review` label + submit requirement from S3 remain present).

## Round-8 remediation
- **HTTP-password generation body (G6):** the `PUT /a/accounts/<bot>/password.http` call MUST send a JSON body `{"generate": true}` (HttpPasswordInput) to trigger server-side generation; the generated password is returned in the response body and captured into SSM `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- The bot's Gerrit HTTP token (SSM `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`) is the receiver's OUTBOUND READ credential for BOTH the REST `/a/` calls (diff fetch + vote) AND git-over-HTTPS clone of the change ref into `repo_root` (S4b's `resolve_gate_handle` context). Its scope must cover both; no separate read key is provisioned (the S5 deploy key is GitHub-write-only).
- `events-log` REST backfill query (`GET /a/plugins/events-log/events`) gets an explicit smoke-check here before S4b's reconciler depends on it (it was not in the named E1–E5 derisk set).

Added ACs:
- [ ] Documented + tested that the bot HTTP token authenticates BOTH Gerrit REST `/a/` and git-over-HTTPS clone of a change ref (the receiver's read path); scope covers both
- [ ] `events-log` query endpoint smoke-checked (returns recent events) as a precondition for S4b's backfill reconciler



## Round-12 plan-review remediation (2026-06-30)
- **Blocking — ADR for inbound-webhook-auth (captured):** `docs/adr/0014-inbound-webhook-auth.md` records the decision: the Gerrit `webhooks` plugin has NO HMAC and reads remote config only from `refs/meta/config`, so (a) the PRIMARY inbound control is the network ACL (`/review/` reachable only from the Gerrit host via nginx/loopback + SG), (b) a URL-embedded secret token is a SECONDARY bounded-exposure control that necessarily resides in `refs/meta/config` (Gerrit-access-controlled, NOT replicated to GitHub via `replicatePermissions=false`, rotatable), and (c) S4b's receiver additionally validates the token + source. Added to Files + an AC.
- **`replicatePermissions=false` durability (advisory):** this is set in S5's replication config; S4a's ADR-0014 notes the dependency (the token's non-exposure to GitHub relies on S5 keeping `replicatePermissions=false`), and S5's AC asserts it.
- **Surgical RMW refs/meta/config push:** `service-user.sh` updates ONLY the `webhooks.config` file in the `refs/meta/config` tree (it writes/replaces that one file and commits), never touching `project.config`/`groups` (S3's label/submit-req/ACL). Verified post-push that S3's LLM-Review label + submit requirement remain.
- **Plugin install rollback:** plugin jars are dropped into the persistent `plugins/` dir; rollback = remove the jar + restart (or `gerrit plugin remove`). Pinned provenance (URL+sha256+version) lets a rebuild reinstall byte-identical; a destabilizing plugin is removed by deleting its jar and restarting Gerrit.
- **Token-generation mechanism (impl reality):** on this PoC's `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` instance, cookie-auth REST mutations are refused, so the bot account + its HTTP token are created via the Gerrit SSH admin command (`gerrit create-account <bot> --group "Service Users" --http-password <generated>`), authenticated by the admin SSH key; the generated password is captured to SSM `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`. (Equivalent end-state to the `PUT password.http {"generate":true}` path; the AC's intent — a generated, SSM-stored bot token + idempotent re-provision — is preserved.)

Added ACs:
- [ ] `docs/adr/0014-inbound-webhook-auth.md` exists and records the no-HMAC / network-ACL-primary / URL-token-secondary / refs/meta/config-resident / not-replicated decision.
- [ ] The refs/meta/config push is surgical (only webhooks.config changes); S3's project.config (LLM-Review label + submit requirement + ACL) is preserved post-push.
